name: CCW Main Orchestrator
description: Main workflow orchestrator: intent analysis → clarity check → workflow category routing

entries.name: default
entries.description: Full flow: intent analysis → clarity check → routing

entries.name: skip-analysis
entries.description: Skip analysis, directly select workflow category

S1.name: Analyze Intent
S1.content_inline:
## Phase 1: Analyze Intent

Analyze the user's input ($ARGUMENTS) to determine:

```javascript
const analysis = {
  goal: extractGoal(input),
  scope: extractScope(input),
  constraints: extractConstraints(input),
  task_type: detectTaskType(input),
  complexity: assessComplexity(input),  // low|medium|high
  clarity_score: calculateClarity(input) // 0-3 (>=2 = clear)
};

const autoYes = /\b(-y|--yes)\b/.test($ARGUMENTS);
```

Detect task_type by priority (use `chain_loader list` to see each chain's triggers):
- bugfix-hotfix, greenfield, brainstorm, brainstorm-to-issue, debug-file, analyze-file
- collaborative-plan, roadmap, spec-driven, integration-test, refactor, team-planex
- multi-cli, bugfix, issue-batch, issue-transition, exploration, quick-task
- ui-design, tdd, test-fix, test-gen, review, documentation, feature (default)

Output: `Type: [task_type] | Goal: [goal] | Complexity: [complexity] | Clarity: [clarity_score]/3`

D1.name: Clarity Check
D1.prompt:
Based on the intent analysis results, assess the clarity score:
1. Clear (clarity_score >= 2) — goal, scope, and constraints are sufficiently defined
2. Unclear (clarity_score < 2) — need more information from user

If auto mode (-y) is active, always choose 1 (Clear) regardless of score.

choices.label: Clear
choices.description: Clarity score >= 2, proceed to workflow selection

S2.name: Clarify Requirements

S3.name: Select Workflow & Build Chain
S3.content_inline:
## Phase 2: Select Workflow & Build Command Chain

Map task_type to workflow flow:

| task_type | level | flow | category chain |
|-----------|-------|------|----------------|
| bugfix-hotfix | 2 | bugfix.hotfix | lightweight |
| greenfield | 3-4 | greenfield-plan/phased | exploration |
| brainstorm | 4 | brainstorm-to-plan | exploration |
| brainstorm-to-issue | 4 | brainstorm-to-issue | issue |
| debug-file | 3 | debug-with-file | with-file |
| analyze-file | 3 | analyze-to-plan | with-file |
| collaborative-plan | 3 | collaborative-plan | with-file |
| roadmap | 4 | roadmap | with-file |
| spec-driven | 4 | spec-driven | exploration |
| integration-test | 3 | integration-test-cycle | cycle |
| refactor | 3 | refactor-cycle | cycle |
| team-planex | Team | team-planex | team |
| multi-cli | 3 | multi-cli-plan | standard |
| bugfix | 2 | bugfix.standard | lightweight |
| issue-batch | Issue | issue | issue |
| issue-transition | 2.5 | rapid-to-issue | issue |
| quick-task | 2 | rapid | lightweight |
| ui-design | 3-4 | ui | standard |
| tdd | 3 | tdd | standard |
| test-gen/test-fix | 3 | test-gen/test-fix-gen | standard |
| review | 3 | review-cycle-fix | standard |
| documentation | 2 | docs | lightweight |
| feature | 2-3 | rapid/coupled | lightweight/standard |
| exploration | 4 | full | exploration |

Route to the appropriate category chain via D2.

D2.name: Route by Workflow Category
D2.prompt:
Based on the task_type detected in Phase 1, route to the appropriate workflow category:
1. Lightweight (Level 2) — rapid, bugfix, hotfix, docs
2. Standard (Level 3) — coupled feature, tdd, test-fix, review, multi-cli, ui
3. Exploration (Level 4) — brainstorm-to-plan, spec-driven, full exploration, greenfield
4. With-File — analyze-to-plan, debug-with-file, collaborative-plan, roadmap
5. Cycle — integration-test-cycle, refactor-cycle
6. Issue — issue batch, rapid-to-issue, brainstorm-to-issue
7. Team — team-planex

Match the task_type from intent analysis to the correct category.

choices.label: Standard (Level 3)
choices.description: coupled, tdd, test-fix, review, multi-cli, ui
